## Working style

Deep work in the mornings (07:00–12:00) — no meetings, no interruptions.
Afternoons for calls, review, and correspondence. I batch email to twice
a day. I work in 90-minute focused blocks with 20-minute breaks.

## Decision-making approach

I prefer to make decisions with sufficient (not perfect) information,
document the reasoning, and revisit if new information emerges. I am
comfortable with reversible decisions made quickly and more deliberate
about irreversible ones. I use structured decision frameworks for
significant choices.

## Tools I use

- **Code**: VS Code with Claude Code extension, Python + uv, Git/GitHub
- **Writing**: Obsidian for notes, iA Writer for long-form, GitHub for
  technical docs
- **Tasks**: Linear for project management, plain text for daily planning
- **Communication**: Signal, email, occasional Slack
- **Hardware**: MacBook Pro M3, mechanical keyboard, external 4K display

## Strong opinions

- MCP is the right abstraction layer for AI tool interoperability
- Local-first is a non-negotiable property for identity infrastructure
- Most software is over-engineered — simplicity is a feature
- Open source with a real maintenance plan beats abandoned "open" code
- AI tools should augment expertise, not substitute for it
